principles: One AWS Organization is the primary governance and security boundary
Production and non-production workloads use separate AWS accounts
Security and audit data are controlled outside workload accounts
Human access is federated through an organization instance of IAM Identity Center
Automation uses short-lived credentials and cross-account roles
The management account contains no application workloads
Service control policies define maximum permissions but do not grant permissions
Account-level cost, security, logging, and backup controls are centrally observable

organizational_units.name: Security
organizational_units.purpose: Central audit, security operations, and immutable log ownership
baseline_controls: Protect organization trails and configuration history
Delegate supported security services from the management account

organizational_units.name: Infrastructure
organizational_units.purpose: Shared platform capabilities that are not owned by a workload team
baseline_controls: Optional hybrid connectivity and shared DNS

organizational_units.name: Workloads-NonProduction
baseline_controls: Lower cost limits and short retention

organizational_units.name: Workloads-Production
baseline_controls: Change promotion only through approved pipelines
Stricter permission sets and separation of duties
Alarm routing and incident response enabled

organizational_units.name: Sandbox
organizational_units.purpose: Time-bounded experimentation without production connectivity or data

organizational_units.name: Suspended
organizational_units.purpose: Quarantine for closed, compromised, or decommissioning accounts
baseline_controls: Require security-owner approval for movement out

accounts.name: management
accounts.purpose: AWS Organizations, consolidated billing, and organization-level administration
accounts.human_access: Break-glass and tightly controlled platform administration only

accounts.name: log-archive
accounts.purpose: Central CloudTrail, Config, access logs, and long-term audit evidence
accounts.human_access: Security audit read-only; restricted log administration

accounts.name: security-tooling
accounts.purpose: Delegated administration for security services and investigation tooling

accounts.name: shared-services
accounts.purpose: Central CI/CD artifacts, package mirrors, shared automation, and optional observability integrations

accounts.name: network
accounts.purpose: Reserved for hybrid connectivity, centralized DNS, inspection, and shared networking if required

accounts.name: cloudops-dev
accounts.purpose: Developer integration environment with synthetic data
accounts.human_access: Developers may administer workload resources within bounded permission sets

accounts.name: cloudops-stage
accounts.purpose: Production-like validation, resilience tests, and release qualification
accounts.human_access: Release engineering and read-only developer access

accounts.name: cloudops-prod
accounts.purpose: Production API, asynchronous processing, data, and operational telemetry
accounts.human_access: Read-only operations by default; emergency elevation is time-bound and audited

accounts.name: sandbox-users
accounts.purpose: Isolated experiments with strict cost and service limits

identity.service: AWS IAM Identity Center organization instance
requirements: MFA enforced at the identity provider
Short session durations for privileged permission sets

rules: Build once and promote immutable artifacts
Each target account has a dedicated deployment role
Production deployment requires protected environment approval
The pipeline role cannot modify organization governance
Application roles cannot assume security or management roles
Rollback and post-deployment verification are mandatory

delegated_administration_candidates: Amazon Macie when data classification requires it

controls: AWS Budgets by account and workload
Organization backup policy when persistent environments exist
Retention derived from RPO and compliance requirements

networking.default_architecture: The serverless workload does not require a VPC for its core managed-service path
networking.network_account_trigger: Use centralized networking only when hybrid connectivity, private endpoints, inspection, or shared DNS is justified
production_requirements: Private connectivity for internal producers when required
Network flow logging where VPCs exist

scp_strategy.purpose: Define maximum permissions and protect governance boundaries; SCPs do not grant permissions
deployment_order: Test in a dedicated policy-staging OU
Apply stricter controls to Security and Suspended OUs
planned_controls: Prevent member accounts from leaving the organization
Restrict unapproved Regions with documented global-service exceptions
Protect organization logging and configuration services
Block creation of long-lived IAM access keys for workforce users
Prevent disabling security services outside delegated administration

production_readiness_gates: Well-Architected P0 and P1 blockers are closed or formally accepted
Authentication, authorization, and restricted CORS are implemented
RTO, RPO, SLO, and ownership are approved
Restore, rollback, and incident-response exercises have evidence
Cost budgets and anomaly detection are active
Central audit and security services are delegated and tested
Production access has separation of duties and protected approvals
DynamoDB Scan is removed from production access paths
